response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned polyglot engineer with strong systems sensibilities — low-latency asynchronous services, event-driven architectures, and a craftsmanship mindset (TDD, mentoring, workshops). However, his hands-on C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, with the last decade dominated by TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms. The role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise plus industrial-cybersecurity grounding (SDL, IEC 62443) and MQTT/IoT protocol work, none of which are evidenced. The seniority and adjacent skills are real, but the core technical match is weak.
strengths: Extensive overall experience (15+ years) spanning embedded, desktop, web, and high-throughput backend systems
Real low-latency, concurrent, event-driven systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Strong mentoring, testing culture, and technical communication track record (workshops, courses, open source)
concerns: C++ experience is dated and brief; recent decade is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not modern C++/STL
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity exposure (SDL, IEC 62443) or MQTT/OPC UA/Modbus protocol work
No industrial automation or IoT domain background; cloud experience is AWS-oriented rather than Azure
